regions.us-ports: Today, winds at Charleston are from the northeast at 11 knots with overcast skies and 0% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions at US East Coast ports including Charleston, Savannah, and Norfolk. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds near 11 knots and mostly overcast conditions will continue in Charleston with only a 2% chance of rain, while similar conditions prevail at Bayonne, Brooklyn, and Manhattan, maintaining steady port operations. By day 3, a 43% rain chance with drizzle and south-southwest winds at 11 knots in Charleston could affect shore excursions and port activities in Charleston, Savannah, and Jacksonville. Long Beach, San Diego, and San Francisco will maintain light winds and dry skies during this period. Beyond 48 hours, monitoring is advised as the increasing drizzle probability in the Southeast US ports may lead to more frequent operational adjustments for cruise vessels and passenger plans.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today in Nassau and surrounding Bahamas ports including Freeport, Bimini, and the Berry Islands, winds are light from the SSE at 4 knots with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, providing ideal conditions for smooth embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to shift to the NE at 11 knots by tomorrow, with overcast skies and a 4% rain chance in Nassau and Key West, Grand Cayman, and Ocho Rios, which will not affect port or excursion operations. By day three, winds will veer to ESE at 11 knots with partly cloudy skies and only a 1% chance of rain affecting Falmouth, Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná, maintaining favorable conditions for cruise movements. No Small Craft Advisories or marine warnings are in effect, so all cruise vessel operations should proceed without weather-related disruption. Passengers and crew should remain aware of the steady but light wind shifts and continuing dry conditions across the Central Caribbean ports over the next three days.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, an east wind at 9 knots will bring a 68% chance of rain showers with overcast skies in San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, and St. Maarten, so we expect rain to impact port operations and shore excursions in these locations. Tomorrow, winds shift slightly east-southeast at 7 knots with an increased 76% rain probability, extending rain showers that are expected to affect San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua, likely causing delays or modifications to embarkation and disembarkation procedures. Beyond 48 hours, the wind returns east at 9 knots with a reduced 48% rain chance, so occasional showers may affect San Juan and nearby ports but with less impact on cruise operations. Visibility remains steady with no advisories for high winds or severe weather, allowing vessels to maintain scheduled arrivals and departures throughout the period. Passengers and crew should plan accordingly for wet conditions during excursions and loading in the affected ports.

Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds will increase slightly to 8 to 9 knots with partly cloudy skies and a low rain chance of 6% on Day 1, followed by drizzle and rain probabilities rising to 22% on Day 2. Passengers and crew should monitor conditions as light rain chances and persistent easterly winds remain steady without significant operational impacts anticipated.

Visibility should remain good despite the drizzle, allowing normal embarkation and disembarkation procedures.
